title: Self-Assessment
content:
Throughout 12 weeks of internship in the First Cloud AI Journey program, I continuously learned and practiced on AWS — starting from foundational concepts, then gradually moving to architecture design, deployment, and operation of the ZeroBug Agent system in a real cloud environment.
In my role developing ZeroBug Agent — an AI platform that automatically generates Unit Tests for software projects — I worked with multiple AWS services including Cognito, API Gateway, EC2, RDS, Bedrock, Lambda, Step Functions, S3, CloudFront, WAF, and CloudWatch. This process helped me gain further experience in cloud architecture design, application security, CI/CD deployment, and applying AI to products.
During implementation, I tried to follow the weekly plan, proactively looked up and resolved technical issues as they arose, and referred to official AWS documentation while sharing and learning from the community.
Below is my self-assessment based on the following criteria, to reflect on my participation in the program honestly and systematically:
No. Criteria Description Good Fair Average 1 Professional knowledge & skills AWS knowledge; applying it to design the ZeroBug Agent system; product quality ☐ ✅ ☐ 2 Ability to learn Absorbing new knowledge about Cognito, API Gateway, CloudFront, and WAF in a short time ☐ ✅ ☐ 3 Proactiveness Self-researching, proposing architecture ideas, and offering technical solutions without waiting for guidance ☐ ✅ ☐ 4 Sense of responsibility Completing worklogs on time and registering the required number of learning sessions and events as specified ✅ ☐ ☐ 5 Discipline Maintaining a regular study and practice schedule each week throughout the 12 weeks ✅ ☐ ☐ 6 Progressive mindset Willingness to refactor code when finding better solutions; learning AWS best practices ☐ ✅ ☐ 7 Communication Presenting system architecture and technical results clearly ☐ ✅ ☐ 8 Teamwork Working effectively when discussing technical solutions and integrating backend/frontend ✅ ☐ ☐ 9 Professional conduct Team unity and respect for everyone in the work environment ✅ ☐ ☐ 10 Problem-solving skills Analyzing and resolving errors that arose during the process of deploying the system to AWS ☐ ✅ ☐ 11 Contribution to project Successfully designing and deploying the entire ZeroBug Agent system on the AWS Cloud platform ✅ ☐ ☐ 12 Overall General evaluation of the entire First Cloud AI Journey program participation ☐ ✅ ☐ Needs Improvement Professional knowledge & skills: Deepen AWS knowledge further, especially in designing the project architecture diagram. Ability to learn: Shorten the time to get familiar with new services such as Cognito, API Gateway, CloudFront, and WAF by systematizing study materials and documenting deployment procedures. Proactiveness: Propose technical and architecture solutions earlier, rather than only addressing issues when requirements arise or obstacles are encountered. Progressive mindset: Regularly review and refactor configuration and source code according to AWS best practices, rather than stopping at a “good enough to run” level. Communication: Present system architecture and technical results more coherently and accessibly — even for those less familiar with cloud computing. Problem-solving skills: Improve error analysis, CloudWatch log interpretation, and systematic incident handling during AWS deployment.

title: Sharing and Feedback
content:
Overall Evaluation 1. Program Quality
The 12-week roadmap is logically structured, progressing from fundamentals (Free Tier, IAM, EC2, VPC, RDS, S3) to advanced services (Bedrock, Lambda, Step Functions, Cognito, API Gateway, CloudFront, WAF). This helped me absorb knowledge step by step at a basic level without feeling overwhelmed by the scale of the AWS ecosystem. Combining theory with hands-on workshops allowed me to both understand concepts and practice directly on the real console.
2. Documentation and Learning Resources
Program materials are presented clearly, with diagrams and illustrations supporting each step. Labs on awsstudygroup.com are updated to match the current AWS console. However, for some newer or less common services — such as Amazon Bedrock AgentCore — Vietnamese documentation is still limited, so I often had to cross-reference AWS documentation in English.
3. Relevance to the Real-World Project
Knowledge from weeks 1 through 8 (IAM, EC2, RDS, S3, CloudFront, etc.) connects directly to the ZeroBug Agent architecture, so I could see the purpose of each service rather than learning in isolation. This structure bridges learning and real deployment well — each week’s content could be applied back to the team project.
4. Opportunities for Practice and Personal Growth
After 12 weeks, I went from being new to AWS to participating in designing and deploying a reasonably stable cloud system. The experience of integrating AWS infrastructure into the team project helped me understand the AWS ecosystem more deeply.
5. Challenges and Memorable Lessons
Biggest technical challenge: Using a Free Tier account limited access to certain AWS infrastructure — for example, not being able to register a domain for Route 53 in the project. There are still many infrastructure components I have not fully mastered. Most important lesson: After using AWS resources, delete everything and carefully review Bills to avoid unexpected charges. 6. Support Policies and Benefits
The $200 AWS Credit package made hands-on practice easier with less worry about unexpected costs. Guidance on setting up Budgets and Billing Alerts from the start was also very helpful — I felt confident trying new services while still controlling spending.
Additional Questions What did you find most satisfying about participating in the program?
→ Working on real AWS and completing a product that can go into my portfolio, rather than only doing standalone labs.
What do you think the program should improve?
→ There should be more direct Q&A channels (livestream Q&A or office hours) to resolve complex errors quickly when deploying the project.
If recommending to a friend, would you suggest they join? Why?
→ Yes. The program suits anyone who wants to learn Cloud and AI in a hands-on way, especially IT students who need real deployment experience for their CV.
Suggestions and Expectations There should be more events for beginners. Yes, I would like to continue this program in the future to learn more about AWS infrastructure.
